content:
```markdown
# Test Chlng Design Document

## Problem Summary
The current system lacks clarity in its functionality, leading to insufficient details for effective action. This ambiguity hinders development and testing processes.

## Proposed Approach
We will implement a structured framework to clarify system functionalities. This will involve defining clear requirements, enhancing documentation, and establishing a feedback loop for continuous improvement.

## File-Level Plan
- **Module A (Owner: Team Alpha)**: Responsible for core functionalities.
- **Module B (Owner: Team Beta)**: Handles user interactions and UI components.
- **Module C (Owner: Team Gamma)**: Manages data storage and retrieval.

## API / Interface Changes
- Introduce new endpoints for enhanced data retrieval.
- Update existing APIs to include detailed response structures.
- Ensure all changes are backward compatible to avoid breaking existing integrations.

## Edge Cases
- **Key Risks**:
  - Ambiguity in requirements may lead to misinterpretation.
  - Potential performance issues with new API endpoints under heavy load.
  
- **Fallback Options**:
  - Revert to previous API versions if new implementations fail.
  - Conduct thorough load testing before full deployment.

## Acceptance Checklist
- [ ] All modules have updated documentation.
- [ ] New API endpoints are functional and tested.
- [ ] Existing APIs maintain backward compatibility.
- [ ] User feedback is collected and addressed.
- [ ] Performance benchmarks are met under load testing.
- [ ] Edge cases are documented and handled appropriately.
```
